title: US Treasury sanctions Chinese oil terminal and Iranian exchange firms in coordinated enforcement
summary: The US State Department sanctioned China-based Qingdao Haiye Oil Terminal for importing sanctioned Iranian crude, while Treasury designated three Iranian foreign currency exchange firms to stem Tehran's financial lifelines.
contents:
## Coordinated US Sanctions Target Iranian Oil Revenue and Financial Networks

### Chinese Entity Designated
The US Department of State imposed new sanctions on entities linked to Iranian petroleum exports, targeting China-based Qingdao Haiye Oil Terminal. The facility stands accused of importing millions of barrels of sanctioned Iranian crude oil and enabling billions of dollars in revenue for Tehran. Beijing immediately rejected the measures as unlawful "unilateral sanctions."

### Iranian Financial Targets
The US Treasury Department simultaneously designated three Iranian foreign currency exchange firms in a separate action aimed at cutting Tehran's "financial lifelines." The firms facilitated currency transactions that enabled the Iranian government to circumvent existing sanctions frameworks.

### Enforcement Context
The coordinated enforcement actions come as the US maintains a naval blockade of Iranian ports that has prevented an estimated $6 billion in oil exports. Iran's economy faces severe pressure with inflation reaching 53.7% according to national statistics, up from 45% before the conflict began.

### Cross-Jurisdictional Implications
The targeting of Chinese-based entities represents an escalation in secondary sanctions enforcement, potentially straining US-China relations ahead of the planned Trump-Xi summit on May 14-15. The EU has also increased scrutiny of Iranian sanctions evasion networks operating through third-country intermediaries.

**Source:** Al Jazeera, State Department announcements
